fix: pre-create all TLD partitions at worker startup to prevent DDL/DML lock deadlock

Running CREATE TABLE ... PARTITION OF domain during the hot-path (concurrent
with INSERT batches from another container) requires ACCESS EXCLUSIVE on the
parent domain table, which queued behind ROW EXCLUSIVE locks from ongoing
INSERTs — causing a full deadlock that stalled both containers.

Fix: _prewarm_all_partitions() runs at main() startup before run_sync_cycle(),
creating any missing partitions while no concurrent ingest is active. From that
point, ensure_partition() inside the per-TLD hot-path is always a no-op
(partition already exists), so no DDL ever contends with live INSERT batches.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

When Cookie Clicker's #prompt modal popup is visible, the overlay
blocks clicks on elements outside the modal. The sight() function now
detects whether #prompt is visible and, if so, restricts the clickable
element search to within the modal only. This ensures the random click
lottery only considers elements that can actually be clicked.

Fix view toggle missing from mobile overflow menu on iOS

The card/list view toggle was not rendering in the SecondaryActions
dropdown on MAUI Blazor Hybrid (iOS WebView). Root cause: inline
ternary expressions in Razor markup (@onclick and @() class/text
bindings) caused the MAUI WebView Blazor renderer to silently drop
the entire <li> element from the render tree.

Fix: extract the ternary logic into computed properties
(ViewToggleIcon, ViewToggleLabel) and a ToggleViewMode method,
keeping the markup simple enough for the WebView renderer.

Verified on iOS 26.2 simulator via DevFlow CDP inspection —
the menu now shows all items including 'List View' toggle.

commit.message:
Fix infinite re-render loop in useKV hook

The parsed value from JSON.parse() created new object/array references
on every render. When App.tsx's useEffect depended on these values
(variantFilters, selectedPokemon, etc.), each re-render saw 'changed'
dependencies, causing setState -> re-render -> infinite loop.

Fix: memoize parsed value with useMemo keyed on the raw string from
localStorage, so refs stay stable across renders. Also fix the init
effect to use a ref for defaultValue instead of the prop (which may
be an inline literal creating new refs each render).

commit.message:
Fix misleading doc on resource_task! and batch_resource_task! macros

The doc comment incorrectly stated that run() checks the guard expression
and returns TaskResult::NotApplicable when it fails. In practice, run()
only checks whether items is empty — the guard is checked by should_run()
and run_if_applicable(), not by run() itself.

Corrected both macro doc comments to accurately describe the run()
behaviour and added a Note directing callers to consult should_run() first,
as execute() does.

Implement UMDF feed synchronization state machine

State machine: WaitInstrumentDefinition → WaitSnapshot → CatchUp → RealTime
- Buffer incremental packets while consuming InstrDef and Snapshot cycles

- Detect snapshot cycle boundaries via SeqNum=1 wrap-around
- Track LastMsgSeqNumProcessed from SnapshotFullRefresh_Header_30
- On catch-up: discard queued incrementals with SeqNum <= snapshot, apply rest
- On gap in RealTime: transition to Recovery → re-consume snapshot
- ChannelHandler.HandlePacket now returns GapResult for caller decisions
- Console app: Ctrl+C support, WaitForCompletionAsync, order logging

fix(#113): fix long-press copy inconsistency on assistant messages (#116)

* fix(#113): fix long-press copy inconsistency on assistant messages

Replace combinedClickable on Surface with pointerInput(detectTapGestures)
on the outer Box container. combinedClickable uses PointerEventPass.Main
(bottom-up), so ClickableText composables inside MarkdownContent (which
also use Main pass) intercepted the gesture first and prevented the
long-press from being detected reliably on tall/rich assistant messages.

detectTapGestures calls awaitFirstDown(requireUnconsumed = false), so
the outer Box receives the down event regardless of child consumption.
Long-press is detected via a delay timeout — independent of whether
inner ClickableText nodes later consume pointer events.

User messages (plain Text, no ClickableText) were unaffected; this fix
makes assistant message long-press parity consistent across all content.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: use CJS output format for non-ESM built-in extensions (#62)

The transpileExtensions() function in build/next/index.ts was hardcoding
'esm' format for all built-in extensions. This caused CJS extensions
(emmet, npm, merge-conflict, etc.) to output ESM import statements,
which then failed at runtime with 'Cannot use import statement outside
a module' when loaded via require().

Now each extension's package.json is read to determine the correct
output format: extensions with "type": "module" (only github) use ESM,
all others use CJS. This matches the gulp ESBuildTranspiler behavior.

Fixes #57

commit.message:
feat: add ResponseSanitizationFilter to strip stack traces from error responses (#1353)

* feat: add ResponseSanitizationFilter to strip stack traces from error responses

Add a servlet filter that sanitizes error responses to prevent Java stack
traces and internal implementation details from leaking to clients.

When an error response (HTTP 4xx/5xx) is detected that contains stack trace
markers, the body is replaced with a generic HTML page showing only the HTTP
status code and a correlation ID. The full original body is logged server-side
with the same correlation ID.

- ResponseSanitizationFilter: captures PrintWriter output via CapturingResponseWrapper,
  detects stack traces via STACK_TRACE_PATTERN, replaces tainted error bodies
- web.xml: registered as outermost filter (first filter-mapping) to intercept
  errors from all downstream layers
- carlos.properties: response.sanitization.enabled=true (default)
- ResponseSanitizationFilterUnitTest: 22 unit tests covering stack trace detection,
  pass-through, sanitization, committed responses, and uncaught exceptions

Compliance: NIST SP 800-53 SI-11, OWASP ASVS 7.4.1, CWE-209

feat(benchmarks): add conv_cifar_v3 suite for phase-3 GPU sweep

15 configurations in 7 groups targeting beyond 75% CIFAR-10 accuracy:

1. Random crop augmentation (pad-4 reflect + random crop), the standard
   CIFAR-10 augment used by CNN baselines, as single-model baseline.
2. n_augment sweep (2, 3 copies per batch) — accumulates more
   augmented variants into the normal-equation system.
3. 20k neurons + crop+flip augmentation (single model).
4. Ensemble ×5 + crop+flip with n_augment sweep.
5. Large ensembles (×10, ×20) with hflip and crop+flip.
6. Diverse patch-size ensembles (p3/p5/p7) ×6, ×9 with crop+flip.
7. Combined best: 20k neurons × 5 ensemble + crop+flip.
